course_description:
Research project. Students completing the course in a single semester must enrol in MRES7018. Students completing the course over 2 semesters must enrol in MRES7019 (start semester 1, finish semester 2) or MRES7020 (start semester 2, finish semester 1.

This course is offered as part of the Master of Magnetic Resonance Technology.

A two week residential component at St Lucia campus is required for this course. The timetabling of this will be dependent upon the individual student’s project and will be decided in conjunction with the student’s supervisor early in the semester.

This is a 6 unit research project offered by the Centre, reflecting the importance of the residential component offering practical experience in the area of imaging.

restrictions: Permission required from Centre for Advanced Imaging. A GPA of 5 or above is required or as agreed by the Course Coordinator.
Another instance of the same course
This course has internal and external enrolment.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
A two week residential component at St Lucia campus is required for this course. The timetabling of this will be dependent upon the individual student’s project and will be decided in conjunction with the student’s supervisor early in the semester.
Tutorials are held at the Centre for Advanced Imaging, Building 57, Level 2 Seminar Room (207).
Please see Blackboard for more information about your timetable.
course_aims: Conduct a research project using imaging.

learning_outcomes.description: Review literature and design a research project using MRI.
Collect, analyse and organise information and convey that information clearly and fluently.
Analyse results, create reports and discuss findings.
Make recommendations and improvements to the field.
